design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed=hash('Super vs Folk|competitor_comparison|people comparing Super with Folk|2026-07-06T00:33:18.572638+00:00') -> hero=comparison-lab, typography=Cabinet Grotesk, components=[decision-matrix, workflow-playbook-timeline, source-card-dossier], gsap=[pinned-chapter-scroll, hover-physics]. AIDA Check: Hero=Attention, Proof grids=Interest, Field guide & motion=Desire, CTA band=Action. Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk (brand-safe). Hero Math Verification: clamp headline spans 2–3 lines at 1120px max-width. Bento Density Verification: compare-grid uses grid-auto-flow:dense. GSAP Motion Selection: guarded ScrollTrigger + hover physics. Label Sweep & Button Check: black primary CTA, white secondary CTA, contrast verified.
headline: Super vs Folk — personal AI agents compared for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Folk. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats.
site_brief.title: Super vs Folk

summary: A fair, source‑backed comparison of Super and Folk for people choosing a personal AI agent. Folk shines for lightweight organization and niche automation. Super is built for repeated computer‑use workflows, with agents that actually operate browsers and desktops and reuse a computer-use cache so work gets faster and cheaper over time. Includes a deep buyer’s guide, evaluation plan, risks, FAQs, and cited market context.
